data.title: Kenya private sector activity contracts for first time in seven months, PMI shows
data.description:
Kenya's private sector activity contracted in May for the first time in seven months, hurt by a slowing performance in the construction, wholesale and retail and services sectors, a business survey showed on Thursday.

The Stanbic Bank Kenya Purchasing Managers' Index fell to 49.6 in May from 52.0 a month earlier. Readings above 50.0 indicate growth in business activity, while those below that signal contraction.

"Total business output contracted at the fastest rate in 10 months in May, although the overall downturn was only slight," Stanbic Bank Kenya said in comments accompanying the survey.

"Declines were generally driven by the construction, wholesale and retail and services sectors, whereas output increased in agriculture and manufacturing."

Business expectations for the next 12 months were subdued in May, falling to their second-lowest level on record. Just 4% of surveyed firms expect improved performance in the period, the survey said.

"Consumers remain hesitant to spend due to concerns about their economic state and the dim outlook," Christopher Legilisho, an economist at Stanbic Bank, said.

Kenya's inflation (KECPI=ECI) fell to 3.8% year-on-year in May from 4.1% a month earlier, data from the statistics office showed.

The economy expanded by 4.7% last year, down from 5.7% in the previous year. The finance ministry forecasts growth of 5.3% in 2025.

data.title: BOC Aviation to Buy Nine Airbus Aircraft, Lease to Avianca in 2027
data.description:
BOC Aviation said it will buy nine Airbus A320NEO aircraft and lease them to Colombian airline Avianca under long-term agreements, including up to six finance leases, according to a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing.

The jets are scheduled for delivery in 2027, the filing added.

data.title: Tatneft plans to expand range of oilfield services, implement digital solutions in Turkmenistan
data.description:
Tatneft and Turkmennebit intend to develop their range of oilfield services in Turkmenistan, Tatneft said in its corporate publication Oil News, as per Interfax."Building on the solid foundation established through 15 years of cooperation, the partners are now looking ahead. Their focus includes further developing service technologies, expanding service offerings, implementing digital solutions and training new specialists," Tatneft said.Since 2012, Tatneft's branch teams have conducted over 350 well interventions in Turkmenistan using downhole pumping equipment, it said. They have mastered drilling technologies for complex geological conditions and established on-site equipment repair and assembly capabilities. New service lines include repairs of electric submersible pump units, drill pipes and sucker rods.The Tatneft-Turkmennebit partnership began in 2008 with downhole equipment supplies to Turkmenistan. In 2010, the companies signed a contract for Tatneft to conduct enhanced oil recovery services at the Goturdepe field. In February 2011, the Russian company registered its Turkmenistan branch.

mrchub.com

data.title: Hon Hai Precision Ind May Rev NT$615.74B Vs NT$550.16B
UK outsourcer Mitie to buy peer Marlowe for $496 million
data.description:
British outsourcer Mitie Group will acquire smaller peer Marlowe for about 366 million pounds ($496 million) in a cash-and-stock deal, the companies said on Thursday, sending the latter's shares up nearly 8%.

Marlowe shareholders will get 1.1 new Mitie shares for each share they hold and 290 pence in cash, implying a value of 466 pence per Marlowe share.

Mitie's shares tumbled 10% to 145 pence, as of 0725 GMT. The stock was the second biggest loser on the FTSE mid-cap index , while Marlowe climbed 8% to 438.5 pence.

The deal represents a premium of 26.5% to Marlowe's closing price on June 3, a day before talks between the companies were first disclosed, according to Mitie.

Marlowe, which has a market capitalisation of about 318 million pounds, provides compliance and risk management services, including safety inspections, to UK businesses.

Mitie provides services such as fire and security as well as water and air hygiene to public and private sector clients.

The company also reported a 11% jump in profit to 234 million pounds for the year ended March 31 and suspended a 125 million pound share buyback programme.

($1 = 0.7381 pounds)

data.title: Kalmar Lands Hybrid Straddle Carriers Order in France
data.description:
Kalmar secured a supply contract for 11 of its hybrid straddle carriers and its MyKalmar Insight performance monitoring system.

French terminal operator Seayard, which made the order, will deploy the equipment at its Seayard Terminal FOS2 XL container terminal in the Port of Fos-sur-Mer in France, according to a Thursday release.

The Finnish cargo handling equipment and services company's order will be delivered in the second quarter of 2026.

data.title: Turkish prosecutors investigate main opposition leader in crackdown
data.description:
Prosecutors have opened an investigation into the head of Turkey's main opposition Republican People's Party (CHP) on charges of threatening and insulting Istanbul's chief prosecutor, adding to a broad crackdown on the opposition.

The clampdown is centred on Istanbul Mayor Ekrem Imamoglu, who is President Tayyip Erdogan's main political rival and leads him in some polls. The CHP's Imamoglu was jailed in March pending trial on corruption charges, which he denies.

The Istanbul prosecutor's office said in a statement that it launched the probe into CHP leader Ozgur Ozel over comments he made about the chief prosecutor at a rally in Istanbul on Wednesday, criticising the targeting of the opposition.

CHP spokesman Deniz Yucel criticised the decision to open the investigation.

"We will not succumb to this system of lawlessness that you have created," he said on X.

Imamoglu's arrest in March triggered mass protests, economic turmoil and broad accusations of government influence over the judiciary and anti-democratic applications. The government denies the accusations and says the judiciary is independent.

Since his arrest, authorities have detained dozens of CHP members, officials from the Istanbul municipality, and other CHP-run municipalities. Access to Imamoglu's X account was also blocked in Turkey this month.

data.title: German Factory Orders Unexpectedly Climb, Overcoming Tariff Concerns — Update
data.description:
By Ed Frankl

German manufacturing orders unexpectedly rose in April, confounding fears that the Trump's administration ratcheting up tariffs would hit demand for German goods.

Factory orders climbed 0.6% on month, Germany's statistics agency Destatis said Thursday, better than expectations of a 1.7% fall from economists polled by The Wall Street Journal.

Nevertheless, the first hard industrial data after President Trump's "Liberation Day" tariff announcement showed a slowdown from the 3.4% increase in March, which came as U.S. firms stockpiled goods from abroad in an effort to get ahead of proposed levies.

Stronger demand at home offset weaker overseas demand, as domestic orders rose 2.2% on month in April, while foreign orders declined 0.3%, Destatis said.

Orders were driven by a 21.5% growth in requests for computers, electronic and optical products, due to several large-scale orders, the statistics agency said. Orders for aircraft, ships, trains and military vehicles also trended 7.1% higher.

The news comes ahead of a meeting of the European Central Bank Thursday. Policymakers are expected to cut its key policy rate, which could free up borrowing restraints for manufacturers in the months ahead. The industry could also get a boost from the new government's increase in infrastructure and defense spending, although that benefit might not arrive until late this year.

The data suggests the industrial sector has passed through recent weakness and that production will expand for the rest of 2025, Commerzbank economist Ralph Solveen said.

"Today's figures increase the chances that the German economy will emerge from economic stagnation in the coming quarters despite higher U.S. tariffs," he added.

German industrial production and trade data for April is due Friday.

Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com

data.title: REG - VinaCapital Vietnam - Daily Net Asset Value
data.description:
RNS Number : 5970L VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fd. 05 June 2025  

VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund Limited

(the "Company" or "VOF")

Estimated Daily Net Asset Value ("NAV") - 04 June 2025

The Company announces that, as at the close of business on 04 June 2025, its unaudited, estimated NAV was USD 965 million or USD 7.10 per share. The Sterling equivalent as at that same date was GBP 711 million or GBP 5.23 per share.

The GBP to USD foreign exchange rate that has been applied to this NAV is 1.3571.

Disclaimers: The estimated daily NAV stated in this announcement has been prepared by the Administrator and reviewed by the Investment Manager, and is based on estimated valuations and, accordingly, the Company accepts no responsibility for the accuracy of the estimated daily NAV. The actual month end NAV in any month may vary from the estimated daily NAVs provided in the relevant month. Accordingly, no reliance should be placed on the estimated daily NAV and it has been provided for indicative purposes only. The Company's unaudited NAV as at the end of each month is published on or around the 10th of each month.

The estimated daily NAV is prepared based on the following valuation guidelines:

•

Listed equities are marked-to-market, based on the closing price of the most recent trading day, provided by the Vietnam stock exchanges (the Ho Chi Minh Stock Exchange and the Hanoi Stock Exchange).

•

Unlisted securities include over-the-counter (OTC) traded securities and unlisted securities publicly traded on the Unlisted Public Companies Market (UPCOM) of the Hanoi Stock Exchange. These holdings are marked-to-market on the same basis as listed equities. Where this valuation approach is not available, unlisted securities valuations are based on the average of up to three independent broker quotes for the last trading day of the prior week, or where these are unavailable, the last available price.

•

Non-publicly traded investments, including private equity and direct real estate investments are valued based on the last available valuation as approved by the Audit Committee, which may be adjusted for any impairment, loss or disposal.

•

The valuation of cash positions is based on cash in the Company's bank accounts at the close of the most recent trading day and is adjusted for receivables and accruals.

•

The Vietnam Dong (VND) foreign exchange rate is the daily reference rate provided by Vietcombank as of the date of the daily NAV. The British Sterling (GBP) and US Dollar (USD) exchange rates are based on the Bloomberg reference rate of the same day.

Further information is available on the Company's website at: https://vof.vinacapital.com/

Enquiries:

  • Joel Weiden

  • Investment Manager - Investor Relations and Communications

    VinaCapital Investment Management Limited

  • T: +84 28 3821 9930

  • E: joel.weiden@vinacapital.com

  • Magdala Mullegadoo

  • Company Secretary / Administrator

    Aztec Financial Services (Guernsey) Limited

  • T: +44 1481 748 814

  • E: vinacapital@aztecgroup.co.uk

1.     References to VOF or the Company in this announcement shall mean VinaCapital Vietnam Opportunity Fund Limited, a non-cellular company incorporated in the Bailiwick of Guernsey under The Companies (Guernsey) Law, 2008, with registered number 61765. It is authorised by the Guernsey Financial Services Commission (reference number 2268242) as a registered closed-ended investment scheme under The Protection of Investors (Bailiwick of Guernsey) Law, 2020 and in compliance with the Registered Collective Investment Scheme Rules, as amended.

2.     The registered office address of the Company is East Wing, Trafalgar Court, Les Banques, St Peter Port, Guernsey, Channel Islands, GY1 3PP.

3.     This announcement contains information that is inside information for the purposes of the UK version of the Market Abuse Regulation (EU) No. 596/2014 which is part of UK law by virtue of the European Union (Withdrawal) Act 2018 (as amended and supplemented from time to time).

data.title: REMINDER: Public Offering of UAB Sostinės Bokštai bonds - via Bond Auction
data.description:
Nasdaq VilniusAnnouncement from the exchange

REMINDER: Public Offering of UAB Sostinės Bokštai bonds - via Bond Auction

Up to 5 000 bonds are being publicly offered to investors in Lithuania, Latviaand Estonia.

Investors in Lithuania, Latvia and Estonia can purchase up to 5 000 bonds witha nominal value of EUR 1 000 and annual interest rate on the nominal valueequal to 6% (fixed) + 6M EURIBOR (variable) per annum.

The purchase price per bond is EUR 1013.9510 (101.39510%).

Essential terms of the offering:

-- The offering period during which the bonds can be subscribed (subscription process) starts on May 19, 2025 at 09:00 EEST and ends on June 06, 2025 at 15:30 EEST as follows:

May 19 - June 05, 2025 from 09:00 until 16:00 (EEST)June 06, 2025 from 09:00 until 15:30 (EEST).Settlement date: June 10, 2025.

-- Retail investor wishing to submit a subscription order must contact their brokerage company.

-- Market: VSE Equities IPO (Genium INET trading system)

-- Order book: HEROSPO6 (ISIN code:

LT0000407629

)

-- All Nasdaq Vilnius Members, having access to Genium INET trading system may participate in the offering by submitting orders on their own account or on behalf of their clients

Auction rules and Prospectus are available as attachments.

data.title: Kazakhstan Holds Key Rate at 16.5%
data.description:
The National Bank of Kazakhstan maintained its benchmark interest rate unchanged at 16.5% during its meeting on June 5, 2025, as widely anticipated, to support the disinflationary process.

The annual inflation rate in Kazakhstan accelerated for the sixth straight month to 11.3% in May 2025, the highest level since September 2023 and remaining well above the bank's target of 5%.

According to the updated forecasts, inflation is now projected to reach 10.5–12.5% in 2025, moderate to 9.5–11.5% in 2026, and decrease further to 5.5–7.5% by year-end 2027.

data.title: REMINDER: Public Offering of UAB Sostinės Bokštai bonds - via Bond Auction
data.description:
Nasdaq TallinnAnnouncement from the exchange

REMINDER: Public Offering of UAB Sostinės Bokštai bonds - via Bond Auction

Up to 5 000 bonds are being publicly offered to investors in Lithuania, Latvia

and Estonia.

Investors in Lithuania, Latvia and Estonia can purchase up to 5 000 bonds with

a nominal value of EUR 1 000 and annual interest rate on the nominal value

equal to 6% (fixed) + 6M EURIBOR (variable) per annum.

The purchase price per bond is EUR 1013.9510 (101.39510%).

Essential terms of the offering:

-- The offering period during which the bonds can be subscribed (subscription

process) starts on May 19, 2025 at 09:00 EEST and ends on June 06, 2025 at

15:30 EEST as follows:

May 19 - June 05, 2025 from 09:00 until 16:00 (EEST)

June 06, 2025 from 09:00 until 15:30 (EEST).

Settlement date: June 10, 2025.

-- Retail investor wishing to submit a subscription order must contact their

brokerage company.

-- Market: VSE Equities IPO (Genium INET trading system)

-- Order book: HEROSPO6 (ISIN code:

LT0000407629

)

-- All Nasdaq Vilnius Members, having access to Genium INET trading system may

participate in the offering by submitting orders on their own account or on

behalf of their clients

Auction rules and Prospectus are available as attachments.

data.title: Iron ore dips as focus shifts to softening China steel demand
data.description:
Iron ore futures dipped on Thursday, as the focus shifted back to softening steel consumption in top consumer China's off-peak demand season.

The most-traded September iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) closed daytime trade 0.14% lower at 701 yuan ($97.60) a metric ton.

The benchmark July iron ore (SZZFN5) on the Singapore Exchange lost 0.8% to $94.7 a ton, as of 0701 GMT.

Given the lack of core driving forces, prices of the key steelmaking ingredient are expected to fluctuate amid seasonally weak demand, analysts at Galaxy Futures said in a note.

"There is no big change to fundamentals in the iron ore market. The wave of upward momentum led by the price rally of coal faded, so ore prices also softened," said Zhuo Guiqiu, an analyst at broker Jinrui Futures.

However, downside potential is capped by a relatively high hot metal output, despite production cuts and declining portside inventories, Zhuo added.

Hot metal output is typically used to gauge iron ore demand.

Despite a trade truce between China and the United States, steel exports have recently shown signs of slumping, dragging demand, Galaxy's analysts added.

Weak steel consumption is also a downside risk for feedstocks.

Other steelmaking ingredients, coking coal NYMEX:ACT1! and coke (DCJcv1), recorded further gains albeit at a slower pace, rising 1.68% and 0.56%, respectively, following Wednesday's more than 6% rally.

Steel benchmarks on the Shanghai Futures Exchange were range-bound. Rebar nudged up 0.14%, hot-rolled coil lost 0.19%, wire rod (SWRcv1) added 0.06% and stainless steel advanced 0.2%.

($1 = 7.1822 Chinese yuan)

data.title: China Warns BYD, Rivals To Self-Regulate As Price War Heats Up- Bloomberg News
data.description: • CHINA WARNS BYD, RIVALS TO SELF-REGULATE AS PRICE WAR HEATS UP- BLOOMBERG NEWS

data.title: Sweco to Purchase Dutch Engineering Group Brain of Buildings
data.description:
Sweco (SWEC-A.ST, SWEC-B.ST) said Thursday it agreed to buy Dutch engineering company Brain of buildings, which will be immediately consolidated into its operations.

The transaction forms part of the Swedish engineering consultancy's strategy to strengthen its market position in Europe's architecture and engineering consultancy sector.

Brain of buildings is expected to report a turnover of 5.2 million euros in 2025.

data.title: Nordex Bags 56-MW Wind Turbine Order in Germany
data.description:
Nordex secured an order for its wind turbines totaling 56 megawatts from Germany-based Umwelt Management, according to a Thursday release.

The German wind company's order covers eight N163/6.X wind turbines, which will be installed at the Drochtersen repowering project in Lower Saxony, Germany, starting in the autumn of 2026. Full commissioning of all turbines will be completed by the first quarter of 2027.

The deal also includes a 20-year service agreement.

data.title: India's RailTel Corp rises on 2.74-billion-rupee order win
data.description:
** RailTel Corp rises 2.6% to 453.5 rupees

** Co wins order worth 2.74 bln rupees ($31.95 million) from the motor vehicles department in the state of Maharashtra to design and operate intelligent traffic management system (ITMS) for 10 years

** Including session's gains, RAIT shares up ~27% in the last 12 months

($1 = 85.7580 Indian rupees)

data.title: Elekta Acquires Assets to Boost Cancer Treatment Services in Croatia
data.description:
Elekta (EKTA-B.ST) acquired assets from its distributor to expand its cancer treatment services and strengthen its position in Croatia.

The deal includes setting up an office in Zagreb, acquiring service engineers, and hiring staff, according to a Thursday release.

The move will help the Swedish radiation therapy group improve its services support, ensure timely maintenance and upgrades, and advanced radiation therapy.

data.title: Norwegian Air Shuttle Logs Higher Passenger Traffic in May
data.description:
Norwegian Air Shuttle said Thursday it carried 2,178,172 passengers in May, compared with 2,144,321 passengers a year ago.

The Norwegian airline's load factor during the reporting month rose to 83.6% from 82.1% in the year-ago period.

The company's Widerøe airline flew 349,345 guests in May, up from 333,514 passengers a year ago, while its load factor dropped to 68.6% from 69.4%.
